commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor real behavior proof and normal maintainer merge handling; there is no narrow automated code defect for ClawSweeper to repair.; Cleared: The diff only touches PDF model-resolution TypeScript and focused tests; it does not add dependencies, workflows, network paths, secret handling, or package metadata. (sha=2e51263e1438c041b1b987c2e20822e5dd227ce3)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes PDF model auto-resolution to prefer authenticated MiniMax text chat model refs for extraction fallback, filters MiniMax VLM refs out of PDF fallback candidates, and updates focused model-config tests.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main skips MiniMax configured chat models in PDF auto-resolution and the current test expects MiniMax-VL-01. I did not run a live MiniMax PDF call.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real behavior proof and normal maintainer merge handling; there is no narrow automated code defect for ClawSweeper to repair.

target.title: fix(pdf): use MiniMax text models for PDF fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: MiniMax-only PDF auto-resolution could select `MiniMax-VL-01`, which is a media-understanding model and not a normal chat model for the PDF tool.
- Solution: Prefer authenticated MiniMax text chat models for PDF extraction fallback and filter MiniMax VLM refs out of PDF fallback candidates.
- What changed: PDF model config resolution now builds MiniMax text extraction candidates from the default model and configured provider catalogs.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Native PDF providers, image tool routing, MiniMax VLM image understanding, provider auth, and runtime secret handling are unchanged.

## Motivation

- Fixes the MiniMax-only PDF path reported in #85575, where the tool can fail before reaching text extraction fallback.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #85575
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: MiniMax-only PDF auto-resolution chose `MiniMax-VL-01`, a media/VLM model, before PDF text extraction fallback could use `MiniMax-M2.7`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, Node 24.8.0. No real provider keys were printed; test auth used redacted/fake `MINIMAX_API_KEY` values.
- Before evidence: Before patch, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts -- -t "does not add configured MiniMax chat models as automatic PDF image fallbacks" --reporter=verbose` passed with the old expectation that MiniMax PDF auto-resolution selected `minimax/MiniMax-VL-01` instead of the configured MiniMax chat model.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.test.ts && pnpm check:changed`
- Evidence after fix: Focused PDF tool regression tests passed: 4 files, 54 tests. `pnpm check:changed` passed with lanes `core, coreTests` including typecheck, lint, import-cycle, runtime sidecar, and guard checks.
- Observed result after fix: MiniMax PDF auto-resolution selects `minimax/MiniMax-M2.7` or `minimax-portal/MiniMax-M2.7` for PDF text extraction fallback and no longer returns MiniMax VLM refs as PDF fallback candidates.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: PDF auto-resolution reused image media provider candidates for MiniMax, so it could choose `MiniMax-VL-01` even though the PDF runtime resolves candidates through the chat model registry before extraction fallback.
- Missing detection / guardrail: The prior regression test locked in the MiniMax VLM selection instead of asserting that PDF fallback should use MiniMax text chat models.
- Contributing context (if known): MiniMax chat and media-understanding models are intentionally separate; PDF fallback needs the chat model path when using extracted text.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: MiniMax configured/default text chat models are selected for PDF extraction fallback, including the `minimax-portal/MiniMax-M2.7` default-model path from the issue.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug is in PDF model config selection before provider calls or PDF parsing, so unit coverage catches the broken decision without requiring live MiniMax credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Adjacent `src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.test.ts` covers the non-native extraction fallback runtime path.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users with MiniMax configured can use the PDF tool through extracted text fallback instead of failing early on a MiniMax VLM model lookup.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
MiniMax PDF auto-select -> MiniMax-VL-01 -> chat model registry lookup -> Unknown model

After:
MiniMax PDF auto-select -> MiniMax-M2.7 -> PDF text extraction fallback -> chat completion
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- Security/runtime controls unchanged: provider auth checks, model registry lookup, PDF file loading limits, SSRF policy, and runtime API-key resolution all stay on the existing paths.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 24.8.0, local source checkout
- Model/provider: MiniMax API-key and MiniMax portal model-selection paths using fake/redacted test auth
- Integration/channel: None
- Relevant config (redacted): `agents.defaults.model.primary = "minimax-portal/MiniMax-M2.7"`; `models.providers.minimax-portal.models = []`

### Steps

1. Configure MiniMax as the default PDF-capable model path.
2. Resolve PDF model config for the PDF tool.
3. Confirm the selected model is the MiniMax text chat model, not `MiniMax-VL-01`.

### Expected

- PDF fallback uses `MiniMax-M2.7` text chat models for extracted PDF text.

### Actual

- After this patch, the resolver returns `minimax/MiniMax-M2.7` or `minimax-portal/MiniMax-M2.7` for the covered MiniMax cases.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: configured MiniMax API-key text model, MiniMax portal default model, existing non-native PDF extraction fallback tests, and changed core type/lint/guard checks.
- Edge cases checked: MiniMax VLM refs are filtered out of PDF fallback candidates so a fallback does not retry the old media-only model path.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: MiniMax PDFs that contain only scanned images still need a tested OCR/VLM PDF path.
  - Mitigation: This PR keeps scope to text extraction fallback and leaves MiniMax image/VLM behavior unchanged for the image tool.

## Out-of-scope follow-ups

- Add a dedicated MiniMax VLM PDF/OCR path only after the PDF runtime can call the media-understanding provider directly.
- Add live MiniMax OAuth PDF proof when credentials are available in a safe test lane.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
target.author: neeravmakwana
